stories.name: WithBackground
WithSlotPropsCustomization
stories.description: Example demonstrating slot props customization. Both autocomplete and text field components can be customized.

inputs-autocomplete.import:
import { Autocomplete } from "@pautena/react-design-system";
import Box from "@mui/material/Box";

example: ```tsx // Basic usage <Autocomplete label="Country" options={countries} onChangeValue={handleChange} /> // With slot customization <Autocomplete label="Country" options={countries} slots={{ textField: CustomTextField }} slotProps={{ autocomplete: { size: "small" }, textField: { variant: "filled" } }} /> ```

inputs-autocomplete.description: Autocomplete component that wraps the MUI Autocomplete component with additional props. Supports extensive customization through slots and slotProps.

label.description: The label for the autocomplete input.

helperText.description: Optional helper text to display below the input.

color.description: Optional color for the autocomplete component.

fetching.description: If `true`, indicates that data is being fetched.

onChangeValue.description: Optional callback function that is called when the value changes.

data-display-board.import:
import { Board } from "@pautena/react-design-system";
import Typography from "@mui/material/Typography";
data-display-board.description: Board component that displays content within a styled Paper component. It supports rendering an array of content lines or a single content string. Additionally, it provides a button to copy the content to the clipboard.

reactDocgen.description:
Board component that displays content within a styled Paper component.
It supports rendering an array of content lines or a single content string.
Additionally, it provides a button to copy the content to the clipboard.

content.description: The content to be displayed on the board. It can be a single string or an array of strings.

spacing.description: Optional spacing value for the board. It can be one of the values: 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5.

sx.description: Optional styling properties for the board, using the theme's styling system.

children.description: Optional children to render instead of the content

dialogs-bootstrapdialog.name: BootstrapDialog

stories.name: Default
AcceptableAndCancelable
WithExtraActions
LoadingWithAcceptable
Loading
Disabled
CustomTexts
WithSlotPropsCustomization

dialogs-bootstrapdialog.import:
import { BootstrapDialog, StoryDialogManager } from "@pautena/react-design-system";
import DialogContentText from "@mui/material/DialogContentText";

example: ```tsx // Basic usage <BootstrapDialog open={true} title="Confirm Action" onClose={handleClose} acceptable cancelable > Are you sure? </BootstrapDialog> // Custom close button <BootstrapDialog title="Settings" slotProps={{ closeButton: { color: 'error' } }} /> ```

dialogs-bootstrapdialog.description: A customizable dialog component. Use it as a base to create more dialogs Supports extensive customization through slots and slotProps. Each internal element can be customized or replaced.

loading.description:
Whether the dialog is in a loading state.
@default false

disabled.description:
Whether the dialog is disabled.
@default false

disableAccept.description:
Whether the accept button is disabled.
@default false

disableCancel.description:
Whether the cancel button is disabled.
@default false

cancelable.description:
Whether the dialog is cancelable.
@default true

acceptable.description:
Whether the dialog is acceptable.
@default true

callCloseWhenCancel.description:
Whether to call the onClose function when the cancel button is clicked.
@default true

actions.description: Actions to be displayed in the dialog.

onClose.description: Function to call when the dialog is closed.

onCancel.description: Function to call when the cancel button is clicked.

onAccept.description: Function to call when the accept button is clicked.

component.description: Component to be rendered inside the dialog.

componentProps.description: Props to be passed to the component.

children.description: Children to be rendered inside the dialog.

slots.description: Optional slots for custom rendering within the dialog

dialogs-confirmdialog.description: ConfirmDialog component renders a dialog to ask a user to confirm an action with customizable title, confirmation and cancellation actions. It optionally requires a passphrase to enable the confirmation action.

ConfirmDialog component renders a dialog to ask a user to confirm an action
with customizable title, confirmation and cancellation actions.
It optionally requires a passphrase to enable the confirmation action.

confirmText.description:
Text to display on the confirm button.
@default "Confirm"

cancelText.description:
Text to display on the cancel button.
@default "Cancel"

onCancel.description: Callback function to handle cancel action.

onConfirm.description: Callback function to handle confirm action.

layouts-drawerlayout.description: DrawerLayout component provides a layout structure with a drawer and an app bar. It allows customization of the drawer, app bar, and drawer content through slots and slot properties.

reactDocgen.description:
DrawerLayout component provides a layout structure with a drawer and an app bar.
It allows customization of the drawer, app bar, and drawer content through slots and slot properties.

clipped.description:
Whether the drawer is clipped under the app bar
@default true

drawerWidth.description: Width of the drawer in pixels

variant.description:
Variant of the drawer behavior
- temporary: slides over content, closes on backdrop click
- persistent: pushes content when open
- mini: shows icons only when collapsed
@default "temporary"

selectedItemId.description: ID of the currently selected navigation item

LinkComponent.description:
The component used to render a link when the `href` prop is provided.
@default 'a'

onStateChange.description:
Callback fired when the drawer state changes
@param newState - The new drawer state

title.description: The title of the drawer layout.

elements.name: intersection
signature

navigation.description: The navigation configuration for the drawer.

slots.description: Optional slots for custom rendering within the drawer layout.

A generic form dialog component that wraps a BootstrapDialog and handles form submission.
Put your form inside and receive the data validated using the input name field.

submitText.description:
The text to display on the submit button.
@default "Submit"

cancelText.description:
The text to display on the cancel button.
@default "Cancel"

onCancel.description: Callback function to be called when the cancel button is clicked.

onSubmit.description:
Callback function to be called when the submit button is clicked.
@param data - The data to be submitted.

data-display-groupvaluecard.name: GroupValueCard

stories.name: Default
stories.snippet:
const Default = () => <GroupValueCard title="Hello world" subtitle="Lorem ipsum sit amet">{[
      <ValueItem key="1" size={{ xs: 12, sm: 6, md: 4 }}>
        <ValueText label="Hello world" value="Lorem ipsum sit amet" />
      </ValueItem>,
      <ValueItem key="2" size={{ xs: 12, sm: 6, md: 2 }}>
        <ValueBoolean label="Enabled" value />
      </ValueItem>,
      <ValueItem key="3" size={{ xs: 12, sm: 6, md: 3 }}>
        <ValueText label="Quantity" value="1200" />
      </ValueItem>,
      <ValueItem key="4" size={{ xs: 12, sm: 6, md: 3 }}>
        <ValueText label="Currency" value="EUR" />
      </ValueItem>,
      <ValueItem key="5" size={{ xs: 12, sm: 6, md: 6 }}>
        <ValueText
          label="I am Batman"
          value=" Does it come in black? It's ends here. Hero can be anyone"
        />
      </ValueItem>,
      <ValueItem key="6" size={{ xs: 12, sm: 6, md: 3 }}>
        <ValueText label="Status" value="Open" />
      </ValueItem>,
      <ValueItem key="7" size={{ xs: 12, sm: 6, md: 3 }}>
        <ValueText label="Level" value="2144" />
      </ValueItem>,
      <ValueItem key="8" size={{ xs: 12, sm: 6, md: 3 }}>
        <ValueRating label="Rating" value={3} />
      </ValueItem>,
      <ValueItem key="9" size={{ xs: 12, sm: 6, md: 3 }}>
        <ValueDatetime
          label="Created at"
          value={new Date(2022, 5, 1)}
          format="yyyy-MM-dd"
        />
      </ValueItem>,
    ]}</GroupValueCard>;

data-display-groupvaluecard.import:
import {
    GroupValueCard,
    TablePlaceholder,
    ValueBoolean,
    ValueContent,
    ValueDatetime,

    ValueRating,

} from "@pautena/react-design-system";
data-display-groupvaluecard.description: GroupValueCard component renders a card with a title, subtitle, and children content. It supports customization for dense layout and centered content.

reactDocgen.description:
GroupValueCard component renders a card with a title, subtitle, and children content.
It supports customization for dense layout and centered content.

title.description: The main title of the card.

subtitle.description: An optional subtitle for the card.

centered.description: If true, the content will be centered.

children.description: The children elements to be rendered inside the card.

dense.description: If true, the card will have a denser layout.

    title={(<Box display="flex" flexDirection="row" alignItems="center">

    subtitle={(<Box display="flex" flexDirection="row" alignItems="center">
      <Typography variant="body2">Dolor sit amet</Typography>
      <Label variant="error" text="since yesterday" sx={{ ml: 1 }} />
    </Box>)} />;

stories.name: ColorInherit
ColorPrimary
ColorSecondary
ColorDefault
ColorTransparent
WithSubheader
stories.snippet: const WithSubheader = () => <Header title="Lorem ipsum" subtitle="Dolor sit amet" />;

stories.name: WithBreadcumbs
stories.snippet: const WithBreadcumbs = () => <Header title="Lorem ipsum" breadcrumbs={breadcrumbs} />;

stories.name: WithActions
stories.snippet:
const WithActions = () => <Header
    title="Lorem ipsum"
    subtitle="Dolor sit amet"
    preset="default"
    tabsMode="panel"
    breadcrumbs={breadcrumbs}
    actions={actions} />;

stories.name: WithPanelTabs
stories.snippet:
const WithPanelTabs = () => (
  <TabProvider>
    <Header
      title="Lorem ipsum"
      subtitle="Dolor sit amet"
      tabsMode="panel"
      tabs={tabs}
    />
    <Content>
      <TabPanel index={0}>
        <Typography>Panel Tab 1</Typography>
      </TabPanel>
      <TabPanel index={1}>
        <Typography>Panel Tab 2</Typography>
      </TabPanel>
      <TabPanel index={2}>
        <Typography>Panel Tab 3</Typography>
      </TabPanel>
    </Content>
  </TabProvider>
);

stories.name: NavigationButton
WithBorder
ColorPaper
CustomActionStyles
WithSlotPropsCustomization

example: ```tsx // Basic usage <Header title="Dashboard" subtitle="Welcome back" breadcrumbs={[{id: '1', text: 'Home', link: '/'}]} actions={[{id: 'add', text: 'Add', onClick: handleAdd}]} /> // Custom breadcrumb separator <Header title="Settings" breadcrumbs={breadcrumbs} slotProps={{ breadcrumbs: { separator: '/' } }} /> // Custom action button styling <Header title="Users" actions={actions} slotProps={{ actionButton: { size: 'large', variant: 'contained' } }} /> ```

data-display-header.description: Section used to provide basic information about the page and display main actions Supports extensive customization through slots and slotProps. Each internal element (breadcrumbs, title, actions, tabs) can be customized or replaced.

loadingTitle.description: Show a loading indicator in the title position

loadingSubtitle.description: Show a loading indicator in the subtitle position

preset.description: Color palete used to render the component

breadcrumbs.description:
List of breadcumbs to represent the path to reach
the page that we are

elements.name: signature

actions.description:
List of actions that can be performed by the user.
Each action will be a button in the header.

actionsVariant.description: Variant used to render the actions

tabs.description: If is set, a list of tabs is dispayed at the bottom

tabsMode.description:
The tabs will work using
- a panel container (panel)
- using a the navigation system (navigation).

layouts-headerlayout.name: HeaderLayout

stories.name: Skeleton
DataTable
Tabs
stories.snippet:
const Tabs = () => <HeaderLayout
    title="Lorem ipsum"
    subtitle="Dolor sit amet"
    slotProps={{
      header: {
        breadcrumbs,
        actions,
        tabs,
      },
    }}>(<Box>
      <TabPanel index={0}>
        <Typography>Panel 1</Typography>
      </TabPanel>
      <TabPanel index={[1, 2]}>
        <Typography>Panel 2</Typography>
      </TabPanel>
      <TabPanel index={3}>
        <Typography>Panel 3</Typography>
      </TabPanel>
    </Box>)</HeaderLayout>;

Fetching

stories.name: UsingHook
stories.description: Example using the useRemoteDataTable hook for state management. This hook aggregates pagination, sorting, and filters state.

example: ```tsx const tableState = useRemoteDataTable({ initialPagination: { pageSize: 25 } }); <RemoteDataTable data={items} columns={columns} {...tableState} query={{ fields: queryFields, filters: tableState.filters, onFiltersChange: tableState.onFiltersChange }} /> ```

tables-remotedatatable.description: A table component designed for displaying remote/server-side data with built-in pagination, sorting, and filtering capabilities. This component is fully controlled - all state (pagination, sorting, filters) must be managed by the parent component. For convenience, use the `useRemoteDataTable` hook to manage this state.

data.description: Array of data items to display in the table

elements.name: signature

@default true

getRowId.description: Optional function to generate unique row IDs. If not provided, uses array index

inputs-select.name: Select

stories.name: Default
WithoutFullWidth
SelectLoaded
Loading
Fetching
SizeSmall
WithBackground
WithSlotPropsCustomization
stories.description: Example demonstrating slot props customization. All internal elements can be replaced with custom components and styled.

example: ```tsx // Basic usage <Select label="Country" value={country} onChange={handleChange}> <MenuItem value="us">United States</MenuItem> <MenuItem value="uk">United Kingdom</MenuItem> </Select> // With custom loading indicator <Select label="Status" value={status} loading slotProps={{ loadingIndicator: { size: 24 } }} /> ```

inputs-select.description: A custom Select component that extends the functionality of the Material-UI Select component. This component supports additional features such as loading and fetching states, custom colors, and full-width display. Supports extensive customization through slots and slotProps.

label.description: The label for the select input.

loading.description: If true, the select input is in a loading state.

fetching.description: If true, the select input is in a fetching state.

size.description: The size of the select input.

color.description: The color of the select input.

fullWidth.description: If true, the select input takes up the full width of its container.

children.description: The child elements to be rendered within the select input.

onChange.description: The callback function to handle changes to the select input.

slots.description: Optional slots for custom rendering within the select

animation.description: The animation style for the skeleton card. Can be "pulse", "wave", or false to disable animation.
